themeWords.meaning: Asa; a king of Judah; possibly “healer/physician”
The personal name of Israel’s God, usually rendered “the LORD”

sentenceTheme: King Asa removes idolatry, strengthens Judah, seeks the LORD, and wins a God-given victory over Zerah the Ethiopian with great spoil.

verses.text: So Abijah slept with his fathers, and they buried him in the city of David: and Asa his son reigned in his stead. In his days the land was quiet ten years.

highlightedWords.meaning: David; beloved; second king of Israel
Asa; a king of Judah; possibly “healer/physician”

verses.text: And Asa did that which was good and right in the eyes of the LORD his God:

highlightedWords.meaning: The personal name of Israel’s God, usually rendered “the LORD”
God; deity; the true God or, in plural, gods

verses.text: For he took away the altars of the strange gods, and the high places, and brake down the images, and cut down the groves:

highlightedWords.meaning: Altar; place of sacrifice
Asherah; sacred pole/grove associated with Canaanite worship

verses.text: And commanded Judah to seek the LORD God of their fathers, and to do the law and the commandment.

highlightedWords.meaning: The personal name of Israel’s God, usually rendered “the LORD”
Instruction; law; Torah

verses.text: Also he took away out of all the cities of Judah the high places and the images: and the kingdom was quiet before him.

highlightedWords.meaning: City; town
Judah; praised; the southern kingdom/tribe

verses.text: And he built fenced cities in Judah: for the land had rest, and he had no war in those years; because the LORD had given him rest.

highlightedWords.meaning: Judah; praised; the southern kingdom/tribe
The personal name of Israel’s God, usually rendered “the LORD”

verses.text: Therefore he said unto Judah, Let us build these cities, and make about them walls, and towers, gates, and bars, while the land is yet before us; because we have sought the LORD our God, we have sought him, and he hath given us rest on every side. So they built and prospered.

highlightedWords.meaning: Gate; entrance to a city or space
The personal name of Israel’s God, usually rendered “the LORD”

verses.text: And Asa had an army of men that bare targets and spears, out of Judah three hundred thousand; and out of Benjamin, that bare shields and drew bows, two hundred and fourscore thousand: all these were mighty men of valour.

highlightedWords.meaning: Judah; praised; the southern kingdom/tribe
Benjamin; “son of the right hand”

verses.text: And there came out against them Zerah the Ethiopian with an host of a thousand thousand, and three hundred chariots; and came unto Mareshah.

highlightedWords.meaning: Cushite; Ethiopian
Army; host; warfare

verses.text: Then Asa went out against him, and they set the battle in array in the valley of Zephathah at Mareshah.

highlightedWords.meaning: Asa; a king of Judah; possibly “healer/physician”
Battle; war; warfare

verses.text: And Asa cried unto the LORD his God, and said, LORD, it is nothing with thee to help, whether with many, or with them that have no power: help us, O LORD our God; for we rest on thee, and in thy name we go against this multitude. O LORD, thou art our God; let not man prevail against thee.

highlightedWords.meaning: The personal name of Israel’s God, usually rendered “the LORD”
Name; reputation; authority

verses.text: So the LORD smote the Ethiopians before Asa, and before Judah; and the Ethiopians fled.

highlightedWords.meaning: The personal name of Israel’s God, usually rendered “the LORD”
Cushites; Ethiopians

verses.text: And Asa and the people that were with him pursued them unto Gerar: and the Ethiopians were overthrown, that they could not recover themselves; for they were destroyed before the LORD, and before his host; and they carried away very much spoil.

highlightedWords.meaning: The personal name of Israel’s God, usually rendered “the LORD”
Plunder; spoil; booty

verses.text: And they smote all the cities round about Gerar; for the fear of the LORD came upon them: and they spoiled all the cities; for there was exceeding much spoil in them.

highlightedWords.meaning: The personal name of Israel’s God, usually rendered “the LORD”
Plunder; spoil; booty

verses.text: They smote also the tents of cattle, and carried away sheep and camels in abundance, and returned to Jerusalem.

highlightedWords.meaning: Small livestock; sheep/goats
Jerusalem; the chief city of Israel/Judah
